title: Initialize a frontend app scaffold for the personal homepage
description: Create the project scaffold, package metadata, and runnable local scripts for a single-page personal homepage.
acceptance_criteria: A standard frontend project structure exists in the repository root
The repo contains package metadata and a documented local start command
No application code is placed under the harness directory
verification_steps: Confirm package metadata exists in the repository root
Confirm the documented local start command runs
Confirm application code lives outside harness/

title: Establish the homepage information architecture and section skeleton
description: Create the single-page structure for navigation, hero, about, projects, experience, contact, and footer.
acceptance_criteria: The homepage contains at least hero, about, projects, and contact sections
Section anchors or navigation targets exist for the visible sections
The layout is coherent on desktop and mobile widths
verification_steps: Inspect source and confirm required sections exist
Confirm visible navigation anchors point to valid targets
Confirm the layout remains coherent across viewport sizes

title: Define the initial visual baseline and reusable styling tokens
description: Set the typography, palette, spacing, and core surface treatments for a professional personal homepage.
acceptance_criteria: Global styles or theme tokens are defined in a central location
The homepage uses a consistent visual system across sections
The design direction is intentionally minimal and professional rather than placeholder-default
verification_steps: Inspect the stylesheet for centralized tokens or shared variables
Confirm section styling is visually consistent
Confirm the result avoids raw default framework styling

title: Populate the first version with placeholder content and working CTA targets
description: Use placeholder profile, project, and contact content so the first version is fully navigable before real copy arrives.
acceptance_criteria: No major section is left visually empty
Buttons and links point to valid placeholders, anchors, or external targets
Text clearly signals where the user must later replace placeholder content
verification_steps: Review the page for empty major sections
Click or inspect CTA targets for valid placeholders or anchors
Confirm placeholder copy is explicit rather than misleading

title: Add baseline accessibility and responsive behavior
description: Ensure semantic structure, keyboard-reachable navigation, readable contrast, and mobile-friendly layout behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Semantic landmarks and heading hierarchy are present
Navigation and primary CTA controls are keyboard reachable
No obvious layout breakage appears at narrow mobile width
verification_steps: Inspect the DOM for semantic landmarks and headings
Confirm navigation and primary controls are keyboard reachable
Check for blocking layout breakage on mobile width

title: Update repository instructions for generator handoff and local run
description: Document how to run the app locally and capture unresolved assumptions after Sprint S001.
acceptance_criteria: README or equivalent contains the install and dev commands
Remaining content or asset gaps are explicitly called out
The repository can be resumed by a fresh-context agent
verification_steps: Inspect README for local run steps
Confirm unresolved assumptions are recorded
Confirm a fresh agent could resume from the harness artifacts

title: Define a bold visual direction and premium design system for the homepage
description: Establish a clear aesthetic concept, upgraded typography pairings, color system, spacing rhythm, and surface language that move the homepage beyond a generic starter look.
acceptance_criteria: The sprint starts from one explicit aesthetic direction rather than generic polishing
Typography, color, spacing, and component treatments are defined as a cohesive system
The chosen direction avoids default fonts, generic AI gradients, and template-like styling
verification_steps: Inspect the final stylesheet for a coherent token system
Confirm a named aesthetic direction is reflected in the UI and generator notes
Confirm the design avoids banned generic patterns such as purple-on-white hero gradients and default font stacks
notes: Implemented in Sprint S002 visual redesign.

title: Redesign the hero into a memorable visual centerpiece
description: Rebuild the hero with stronger spatial composition, more intentional hierarchy, and one memorable visual signature that immediately distinguishes the homepage.
acceptance_criteria: The hero uses an intentional composition rather than a standard centered stack
The first screen contains a memorable focal device such as layered forms, editorial framing, or a crafted visual motif
Primary CTA hierarchy remains clear while the hero gains stronger visual impact
verification_steps: Inspect the hero layout on desktop and mobile
Confirm the hero contains a distinct visual motif beyond simple text-and-button stacking
Confirm CTA visibility and readability remain intact
notes: Implemented in Sprint S002 visual redesign.

title: Upgrade section layouts with editorial rhythm, depth, and atmosphere
description: Refine the section compositions, cards, dividers, and backgrounds to create a premium editorial flow with depth, asymmetry, and stronger atmosphere.
acceptance_criteria: At least the hero, projects, and contact sections receive visibly improved layout composition
Background layers, spacing rhythm, and surface treatments create a more immersive page atmosphere
The page no longer reads as a plain stacked template from top to bottom
verification_steps: Inspect section transitions and composition across the page
Confirm there are intentional depth or atmospheric treatments rather than flat single-color blocks
Confirm the layout remains coherent while becoming more visually distinctive
notes: Implemented in Sprint S002 visual redesign.

title: Introduce high-impact motion and interaction polish without scope creep
description: Add restrained but meaningful motion, hover states, and reveal behaviors that reinforce the new art direction while keeping the page lightweight and accessible.
acceptance_criteria: The page uses a few deliberate motion moments instead of scattered generic micro-interactions
Motion supports hierarchy and mood without blocking usability or navigation
Interactive states feel intentional and consistent with the chosen visual direction
verification_steps: Confirm motion appears in high-impact moments such as initial reveals or section transitions
Confirm hover and focus states are visually intentional
Confirm reduced-complexity behavior remains usable on mobile and keyboard navigation
notes: Implemented in Sprint S002 visual redesign.

title: Close obvious polish gaps and document the visual decisions
description: Address obvious polish gaps such as the missing favicon if feasible within the static setup and document the final art direction, design decisions, and remaining content limitations.
acceptance_criteria: The page no longer emits an avoidable favicon 404 if this can be solved within current stack boundaries
Generator notes describe the chosen aesthetic direction, font decisions, palette logic, and signature visual motif
README or notes clearly separate finished polish work from remaining content placeholder work
verification_steps: Check whether favicon requests succeed or a reasoned exception is documented
Review generator notes for explicit visual decision rationale
Confirm remaining content gaps are still documented after the redesign
notes: Implemented in Sprint S002 visual redesign.
